label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this ethical dilemma. So the question is whether advertisers should continue digitally manipulating images in their ads, given the potential harm to consumers' mental health, especially young people. Hmm. First, I need to understand the different sides of this. On one hand, ads are a form of art and marketing, right? Companies want their products to look appealing, and part of that includes the people in the ads. But when they digitally alter images—like making someone thinner, smoothing out skin, changing facial features—it can create an unrealistic standard. That could lead to issues like low self-esteem or even eating disorders. Especially with young people who are still developing their self-image, seeing these perfect images might make them feel bad about themselves. On the other hand, advertisers might argue that these manipulations are just part of the creative process. They might say that it's not their fault if people take the images too seriously.
